sentence: Here is the SOAP note:
Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Stephanie Morales, is a 36-year-old female presenting with ankle pain, which occurred 2 days ago when she slipped on ice and twisted her left ankle on the outside.
She reports pain, swelling, and difficulty walking.
She has tried ibuprofen, ice, and elevation for pain and swelling, with minimal relief.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of diabetes and high blood pressure.
She is currently taking metformin and norvasc.
Review of systems: No pain or symptoms in other organs are reported.
Current medications:
Metformin [dosage NOT MENTIONED] [frequency NOT MENTIONED] [indication: diabetes]
Norvasc [dosage NOT MENTIONED] [frequency NOT MENTIONED] [indication: high blood pressure]
Objective:
Vital signs: Blood pressure 120/70, [temperature NOT MENTIONED], [pulse rate NOT MENTIONED], [oxygen saturation NOT MENTIONED].
Physical exam: The patient has tenderness over the lateral malleolus and the fifth metatarsal, with swelling and redness over the left ankle.
Valgus stressing of the ankle causes pain.
The patient has normal sensation and pulses in the affected area.
Test Results: [No lab results mentioned]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient likely has an ankle sprain, and I am concerned for a possible fracture, which warrants an x-ray.
Problem list: Ankle sprain; possible fracture; diabetes; hypertension
Plan:
I ordered an x-ray of the ankle to rule out a fracture.
I will put the patient in a splint and provide crutches until the x-ray results are available.
I prescribed naprosyn 500mg twice a day for pain control.
I instructed the patient to elevate the ankle, apply ice, and avoid weightbearing until further notice.
I will call the patient if the x-ray shows a fracture and will refer her to orthopedics if necessary.
Follow-up: The patient will follow up with me after the x-ray results are available.
I will also see her in 2 weeks for a check-up and to review the results of the x-ray.
